course_name: Bachelor in Information Technology (Cyber Security) with Honours (BCS) (R/482/6/0184) (06/09/2024 - 05/09/2029)
course_introduction:
The Bachelor in Information Technology (Cyber Security) with Honours programme focuses on systems and network security, data security protection, cybercrime forensic, defense methods and cybersecurity technologies, provides students with a solid foundation in related field. Students will learn how to utilize security tools and systems to prevent, detect, and repair security vulnerabilities. This course will also furnish students with a solid foundation in Information Technology to meet the significant market demand. The course is dedicated to cultivate computer and network security professionals. We aim to assist students in exploring their potential through engaging teaching, research, activities and competitions.
Partnerships:

course_name: Bachelor of Computer Science (Hons) in Software Engineering (BSE) (R2/481/6/0727) (16/12/2025 - 15/12/2030)
course_introduction:
The Bachelor of Computer Science (Hons) in Software Engineering programme offers a 3-year curriculum encompassing the study of programming languages, software development, algorithms, data structures, and the principles of computing, providing students with a solid foundation in Computer Science.
This programme offers specialized tracks designed to equip students for diverse career pathways within the rapidly evolving IT sector. Students have the flexibility to choose from a range of elective tracks, including:
Artificial Intelligence and Robotics Deepen your understanding of AI, encompassing machine learning, computer vision, robotics and embedded systems, along with their practical applications.
Applied Generative AI Explore the innovative realm of generative AI technologies and AI-Generated Content (AIGC), learning to leverage them for creative applications across various industries.
Internet-of-Things (IoT) Dive into the interconnected world of IoT, mastering the technologies that enable the seamless connection of devices, sensors, and data, paving the way for smart solutions and innovations.
Big Data and Data Analytics Acquire essential skills in collecting, processing, visualizing, analyzing, and interpreting large datasets, extracting valuable insights and driving informed decision-making in diverse domains.
Extended Reality (XR) Embark on an immersive journey into extended reality technologies, including virtual reality (VR), augmented reality (AR), and mixed reality (MR), exploring their potential applications and creating interactive experiences.
Through this programme, students will gain both theoretical knowledge and practical skills necessary to excel in the dynamic field of Computer Science, empowering them to pursue diverse career opportunities and contribute to technological innovation.
Partnerships:

The Diploma in Computer Science programme offers a 2-year study encompassing the Computer Science discipline, providing students with a broad spectrum of computer knowledge. The curriculum covers areas such as Programming, Network, Operating System, Computer Architecture, and Mathematics for computer science.
Throughout the programme, students acquire a solid foundation in computing theory and practical skills, along with a broader understanding of Computer Science, preparing them for both current market demands and future professional development.
Upon successful completion of this Diploma programme with a CGPA of 2.5 or above and meeting the credit transfer requirements, you will be eligible to admit into Year 2 of the following degree programmes offered at New Era University College:
Partnership:

The Diploma in Information Technology programme offers a comprehensive 2-year curriculum designed to establish a strong foundation in the IT field. Covering a wide array of essential topics such as programming concepts, networking, database concepts, and web design, this programme equips students with vital skills crucial for success in IT careers.
A key highlight of this programme is its integration of Work-Based Learning (WBL), an approach that bridges academic learning with the industry practice. Through WBL, students not only grasp IT concepts but also acquire practical skills directly applicable to the workplace. The Diploma in Information Technology programme aims to train students to emerge as adaptable and industry-ready IT professionals, well-prepared to thrive in the rapidly evolving information technology field.
Work-Based Learning, or WBL is a method of learning on the job that entails collaboration between educational institutions and industries. This approach integrates classroom learning with real-life practice in real workplace settings. As part of this programme, students engage in a WBL placement with our industry partners during their final semester.
Upon successful completion of this Diploma programme with a CGPA of 2.5 or above and meeting the credit transfer requirements, you will be eligible to admit into Year 2 of  the following degree programmes offered at New Era University College:
Partnerships:

The Foundation in Arts (FIA) Programme at New Era University College (NEUC) is a full-time, one-year programme designed to equip students with the essential skills needed for success across various fields. This programme emphasises the development of critical competencies, including strategic thinking, effective communication, and resource management. Additionally, students will gain practical experience in analysing complex issues, solving real-world problems, and collaborating across disciplines.
The programme offers exposure to diverse fields such as Business and Accountancy, Mass Communication, Design, and Education. In the competitive world of the arts, professionals and executives must demonstrate a broad range of skills, knowledge, and adaptability to thrive in an ever-changing environment. Upon completing this programme, students will be well-prepared to pursue a variety of Bachelor’s degrees at NEUC.
